title: Doyle and Evelyn Zimmerman 50th anniversary announcement, Colby Free Press, 11 Jun 2004
blurb: Family-supplied announcement establishes marriage on 14 Jun 1954 at Sacred Heart Catholic Church, Colby; documents Doyle in Selden until 1953, Evelyn in Winona until 1954, the couple meeting in January 1954, and the Hi-Plains Implement career from 1953 through retirement in June 1999. Also records Evelyn at Winona until the 1954 marriage and working alongside Doyle at Hi-Plains Implement from 1974 until the couple's 1999 retirement. Contradicts the compiled 14 Jun 1953 marriage date.
search_text: Doyle and Evelyn Zimmerman 50th anniversary announcement, Colby Free Press, 11 Jun 2004 Family-supplied announcement establishes marriage on 14 Jun 1954 at Sacred Heart Catholic Church, Colby; documents Doyle in Selden until 1953, Evelyn in Winona until 1954, the couple meeting in January 1954, and the Hi-Plains Implement career from 1953 through retirement in June 1999. Also records Evelyn at Winona until the 1954 marriage and working alongside Doyle at Hi-Plains Implement from 1974 until the couple's 1999 retirement. Contradicts the compiled 14 Jun 1953 marriage date.

title: Evelyn Delores Zimmerman obituary, Baalmann Mortuary
blurb: Names Homer and Marjorie Clemans Mundell; states marriage to Doyle J. Zimmerman; says Evelyn was raised by grandmother Frances Adolph and graduated from Winona High School.
search_text: Evelyn Delores Zimmerman obituary, Baalmann Mortuary Names Homer and Marjorie Clemans Mundell; states marriage to Doyle J. Zimmerman; says Evelyn was raised by grandmother Frances Adolph and graduated from Winona High School.

blurb: Names Homer Claire Mundell in Donald's parent line and places Evelyn Zimmerman with Donald and two other daughters in the same sibling group.

title: The German Catholic Settlers of Waterloo County PDF

title: Thomas A. Nauer, WikiTree
blurb: Compiled lead naming Thomas as son of Johann Lorenz Nauer and Mary Dorothea Wand; use as a target, not proof, because the Waterloo PDF uses Dorothea Meyer, widow Wand/Wandt.
search_text: Thomas A. Nauer, WikiTree Compiled lead naming Thomas as son of Johann Lorenz Nauer and Mary Dorothea Wand; use as a target, not proof, because the Waterloo PDF uses Dorothea Meyer, widow Wand/Wandt.

title: New Prussia, Ontario locality context

search_text: New Prussia, Ontario locality context Wilmot Township settlement context for Rhenish Prussian Catholic families and later movement toward Bruce County; useful geographic context for the Nauer search, not a direct proof record.

title: Standesamt Lebehnke civil registration fond 55/589/0, Szukaj w Archiwach
blurb: Urzad Stanu Cywilnego Lubianka, held at the Poznan State Archive branch in Pila: birth, marriage, and death registers 1875-1930, fully digitized and viewable online without login. Likely the only surviving Lebehnke vital records.
search_text: Standesamt Lebehnke civil registration fond 55/589/0, Szukaj w Archiwach Urzad Stanu Cywilnego Lubianka, held at the Poznan State Archive branch in Pila: birth, marriage, and death registers 1875-1930, fully digitized and viewable online without login. Likely the only surviving Lebehnke vital records.

title: Lebehnke Evangelical parish entry, westpreussen.de church-book survey
blurb: States the parish was attached to Schneidemuehl, Kreis Kolmar, until becoming independent in 1839, and that the church books kept from 1846 onward were destroyed in the Second World War - making the civil registers above the primary target.
search_text: Lebehnke Evangelical parish entry, westpreussen.de church-book survey States the parish was attached to Schneidemuehl, Kreis Kolmar, until becoming independent in 1839, and that the church books kept from 1846 onward were destroyed in the Second World War - making the civil registers above the primary target.

title: Southern Tuolumne County Historical Society genealogy page 213
blurb: Compiled Harvey William Mundell page with John Mundell/Deborah Elmore parentage, Elizabeth Jane Brown marriage, Civil War service, and census trail; use as a pointer to primary records. Site verified July 2026 as the Southern Tuolumne County, California historical society, previously misattributed here to St. Charles County.

title: Harvey William Mundell, FamilySearch

search_text: Henry Stropes, FamilySearch Sibling-index support for John Stropes Sr. and Eleanor Fox as parents in the Stropes family.

title: Henry Claar and Electa A. White family group, Treespot
blurb: Names Samuel Claar and Lydia Stropes as Henry's parents.
search_text: Henry Claar and Electa A. White family group, Treespot Names Samuel Claar and Lydia Stropes as Henry's parents.

title: Daniel White, FamilySearch
blurb: Public index names Abel White and Rebecca Solace as parents.
search_text: Daniel White, FamilySearch Public index names Abel White and Rebecca Solace as parents.

title: Personenstandsgesetz of 6 Feb 1875, full text, Wikisource
blurb: Context-only source: the Reich law making civil registration and civil marriage obligatory from 1 Jan 1876, following Prussia's 1874 introduction; used for the Kulturkampf aside on the Zodrow jurisdiction stack, not as family evidence.
search_text: Personenstandsgesetz of 6 Feb 1875, full text, Wikisource Context-only source: the Reich law making civil registration and civil marriage obligatory from 1 Jan 1876, following Prussia's 1874 introduction; used for the Kulturkampf aside on the Zodrow jurisdiction stack, not as family evidence.

title: Dust Bowl, Kansapedia, Kansas Historical Society
blurb: Context-only source for the 1930s drought and dust storms on the Kansas High Plains, including the 14 Apr 1935 Black Sunday storm; used for the anchors birth-cohort aside, not as family evidence.
search_text: Dust Bowl, Kansapedia, Kansas Historical Society Context-only source for the 1930s drought and dust storms on the Kansas High Plains, including the 14 Apr 1935 Black Sunday storm; used for the anchors birth-cohort aside, not as family evidence.

title: 1851 Canada census, Lorenz Nauer household, Wilmot Township
blurb: Primary census pages 141-144 place Lorenz and Thorothea Nauer, two Wand youths, and four Nauer children in one Prussia-born Roman Catholic Wilmot Township household; Thomas is absent and relationships are unstated.
search_text: 1851 Canada census, Lorenz Nauer household, Wilmot Township Primary census pages 141-144 place Lorenz and Thorothea Nauer, two Wand youths, and four Nauer children in one Prussia-born Roman Catholic Wilmot Township household; Thomas is absent and relationships are unstated.

title: 1837 James Mundell probate index, Clinton County Courthouse Archives
blurb: Official loose-court-paper index identifies John Mundell as a son in James Mundell probate folder 236A, file 32, dated 14 Aug 1837; original papers remain to be pulled.
search_text: 1837 James Mundell probate index, Clinton County Courthouse Archives Official loose-court-paper index identifies John Mundell as a son in James Mundell probate folder 236A, file 32, dated 14 Aug 1837; original papers remain to be pulled.

title: Descendants of James Mundell and His Wife, Margaret (Garrett) Mundell
blurb: Barry and Woodruff's 1968 genealogy transcribes Clinton County Will Book I, page 22, with a bequest to son John and the same 14 Aug 1837 probate date; the older Pennsylvania-to-Kentucky identity remains circumstantial.
search_text: Descendants of James Mundell and His Wife, Margaret (Garrett) Mundell Barry and Woodruff's 1968 genealogy transcribes Clinton County Will Book I, page 22, with a bequest to son John and the same 14 Aug 1837 probate date; the older Pennsylvania-to-Kentucky identity remains circumstantial.

title: 1860 United States census, Zephaniah and Catherine Dible household
blurb: Original census image enumerates Catherine Dible, 21, Pennsylvania-born, with Zephaniah in Allegheny Township on 12 Jul 1860, supporting birth about 1839.
search_text: 1860 United States census, Zephaniah and Catherine Dible household Original census image enumerates Catherine Dible, 21, Pennsylvania-born, with Zephaniah in Allegheny Township on 12 Jul 1860, supporting birth about 1839.

title: 1886 marriage application of Mary E. Dible
blurb: Mary E. Dible's signed Westmoreland County marriage application 628 names her parents Zephaniah and Catherine Dible.
search_text: 1886 marriage application of Mary E. Dible Mary E. Dible's signed Westmoreland County marriage application 628 names her parents Zephaniah and Catherine Dible.

title: History of Wapello County, Iowa, and Representative Citizens
blurb: The 1901 John Dible sketch calls him a son of Zephaniah and Catherine Moore Dible and says Zephaniah later remarried Clarinda Stodghill; it does not establish Catherine's death year or parents.
search_text: History of Wapello County, Iowa, and Representative Citizens The 1901 John Dible sketch calls him a son of Zephaniah and Catherine Moore Dible and says Zephaniah later remarried Clarinda Stodghill; it does not establish Catherine's death year or parents.
